current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run the first proxy comparison for the `jumprelu` family at K=128 using the prototype already added under `sparsify/`. Keep the round focused on establishing whether this family can materially improve the anchor-quality regime before any smaller-K or throughput tradeoffs.

Run the first proxy comparison for the `jumprelu` family at K=128 using the prototype already added under `sparsify/`. Keep the round focused on establishing whether this family can materially improve the anchor-quality regime before any smaller-K or throughput tradeoffs. full

Advance the `jumprelu` family from prototype to stabilization with a single family-specific parameter sweep point at the K=128 anchor. Keep architecture and optimizer fixed and test a sharper thresholded encoder to see whether the current 0.1888 full FVU can be pushed materially lower before considering any cost tradeoffs.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer.

The new JumpReLU family deserves its first proxy measurement at the K=128 quality anchor; learnable thresholds with smooth gating may suppress weak activations and beat plain TopK FVU before any cost-oriented moves.

JumpReLU can reduce FVU further at K=128 by making its gating sharper and more selective; increasing the initial threshold while narrowing the bandwidth should suppress weak activations more aggressively and improve reconstruction quality.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If the sharper JumpReLU setting improves proxy FVU, continue stabilizing within the family by tuning threshold strength before any K reduction.
If sharper gating hurts quality, try a softer stabilization point with moderate threshold and bandwidth rather than abandoning JumpReLU immediately.
If JumpReLU plateaus after a few threshold sweeps, introduce a new routing-focused family instead of continuing weak local search.
